ew_context: Price completing handle pullback after cup formation — likely corrective wave 2 within larger impulse structure from 81.35 low
pattern_desc: Well-defined cup & handle with cup bottom at 83.31 and rim resistance at 84.86. Handle pullback showing controlled selling with decreasing volatility ahead of breakout attempt.
key_levels: Support: 84.14 (handle low), 83.31 (cup bottom). Resistance: 84.86 (cup rim), 85.56 (prior swing high)
invalidation: Close below 84.14 on H4 timeframe would negate the handle structure and signal deeper correction
market_context: Crypto showing mixed momentum as Bitcoin tests 80k acceptance. SOL positioning for breakout despite broader market uncertainty around Fed policy and geopolitical tensions
full_analysis:
## Market Structure & Technical Context

SOL has carved out a textbook cup & handle formation with the cup bottom established at 83.31 and rim resistance clearly defined at 84.86. The handle pullback from recent highs shows controlled selling pressure, with price now positioned at 84.91 just above the critical breakout zone. This pattern represents a continuation structure within the broader uptrend from the 81.35 swing low.

RSI at 62.9% indicates healthy momentum without overbought conditions, while EMA20 at 84.17 sits below EMA50 at 84.39 reflecting recent bearish crossover during handle formation. ATR of 0.765 suggests normal volatility conditions for pattern completion.

## Key Levels

- **Resistance**: 84.86 (cup rim breakout level), 85.56 (prior swing high structure)
- **Support**: 84.14 (handle low and stop loss zone), 83.31 (cup bottom foundation)

## Invalidation Level

Close below 84.14 on H4 timeframe would break the handle structure and signal the pattern has failed, potentially triggering deeper correction toward cup bottom support.

## Macro & Flow Context

Crypto markets remain volatile amid Bitcoin's test of 80k acceptance and mixed institutional flows. CFD physics show laminar flow conditions with momentum alignment supporting the breakout thesis despite recent EMA bearish crossover.
